audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Thessalonians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for prayer, Lord's faithfulness, disorderly idleness, apostolic example, not enemy but admonish as sibling, final peace/grace.

verses.restored: Finally, siblings, pray concerning us, that the word of the Lord may run and be glorified, just as also with you,
and that we may be rescued from out-of-place and harmful humans; for not all have the trust.
But trustworthy is the Lord, who will establish you and guard you from the harmful one.
And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that what we command you, you are doing and will do.
May the Lord direct your hearts into the love of the Divine and into the endurance of Messiah.
Now we command you, siblings, in the name of our Lord Jesus Messiah, to withdraw from every sibling walking disorderly and not according to the tradition that they received from us.
For you yourselves know how it is necessary to imitate us, because we were not disorderly among you,
nor did we eat bread from anyone free of charge, but in labor and toil night and day we worked so as not to burden any of you.
Not because we do not have authority, but so that we might give ourselves as a pattern to you, for you to imitate us.
For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: if anyone is not willing to work, neither let that one eat.
For we hear some among you are walking disorderly, not working but working around/busying themselves in others' matters.
Now such ones we command and appeal in Lord Jesus Messiah, that with quietness working, they eat their own bread.
But you, siblings, do not grow weary doing the beautiful/good.
And if anyone does not obey our word through the letter, mark this one; do not mix together with that one, so that one may be turned/shamed toward correction.
Yet do not consider that one as enemy, but admonish as sibling.
Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace through all, in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
The greeting by my hand, Paul's, which is a sign in every letter; thus I write.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Messiah be with all of you.

translation_notes: The work/idleness instruction addresses disorderly refusal and meddling in this community context; it should not be weaponized against the poor, disabled, exploited, or those unable to work.
Verse 15 is crucial: do not regard the disciplined person as enemy, but admonish as sibling.
Final peace and grace include all of you, preserving the restorative frame.
